meta.name: second_codex_contract_integrity_pass_rccf
meta.purpose: Tighten the first Codex scaffold pass for Job Search OS by verifying exact contract compliance, workflow/status compliance, integrity-rule compliance, and open-issue preservation using the approved MVP sources only.
preferred_behavior: Do not expand scope beyond the approved MVP.
Do not add new routes, screens, or product features.
Make the minimum code changes required to achieve exact source-aligned compliance.

source_of_truth_policy: Use only the approved files in data/foundation and docs as product truth for this pass.
Use the first-pass scaffold codebase itself as the implementation under review and repair.
If current code behavior is not traceable to an approved source, remove it, tighten it, or mark it as TODO rather than inventing logic.
If source ambiguity remains, preserve the ambiguity as unknown, gap, risk, comment, or TODO rather than normalizing it away.
hard_constraints: Do not add authentication, multi-user support, resume upload/parsing, resume generation, cover-letter generation, LinkedIn rewrite flows, dashboards, integrations, scraping, or speculative future features.
Do not broaden the rules engine beyond what is needed for exact alignment to the approved files.
Do not redesign the UI unless a small adjustment is required for contract or workflow correctness.
Do not refactor broadly if a localized fix is sufficient.
Do not rename approved statuses, tracker fields, analysis fields, or source-governed concepts for style reasons.
Do not overwrite original analysis context when updating tracker workflow state.
priority_order: Exact tracker field and status-model compliance
Only then: narrow cleanup needed to support those fixes

context.current_stage: The first-pass MVP scaffold is directionally acceptable, but this pass exists only to verify and tighten exact compliance where the first review graded partial rather than full pass.
context.approved_core_loop: Paste job -> analyze honestly -> choose lane/variant -> decide apply/hold/pass -> save to tracker -> review saved jobs
known_non_goals_to_enforce: product polish beyond what is required for correctness
future architecture work not required for this pass

command.primary_objective: Inspect the first-pass scaffold implementation and make the minimum necessary code changes to achieve exact source-aligned contract, workflow, integrity, and open-issue compliance.
steps: Read the approved source files first.
Inspect the current implementation files, especially the focus modules listed in this RCCF.
Verify that the analysis output shape exactly aligns to 04_analysis_schema.json.
Verify that tracker records, tracker fields, and status values exactly align to 05_workflow_rules.json.
Verify that resume routing and lane routing use only the approved lane and resume-direction sources.
Verify that 07_integrity_rules.json is actively enforced: unsupported claims blocked, unknowns preserved, gaps surfaced, and evidence discipline maintained.
Verify that 09_open_issues.md remains unresolved in behavior where required, especially date/title precision and other ambiguity-sensitive items.
Verify that tracker detail behavior updates workflow state only and preserves original analysis context.
Run build/test verification if the runtime supports it. If it does not, say so explicitly and still complete the source-aligned code pass.
Apply only the smallest justified fixes needed to correct mismatches.
Stop after the contract/integrity pass is complete.

checks: jobPosting validation/normalization respects approved enums and unknown handling
jobAnalysis contains all required output sections from 04_analysis_schema.json
field names and value shapes match approved schema rather than near-equivalents
nextAction values stay inside the approved action set
tracker records contain the required fields from 05_workflow_rules.json
application statuses match the approved status model exactly
networking statuses match the approved status model exactly
status mapping is explicit and centralized
save/update flows do not create unofficial status values
unknown values remain unknown
missing evidence becomes a gap rather than an invented match
strong inference is not presented as confirmed proof
unsupported analyst, technical, enterprise, or implementation depth is not inflated
allowed tool claims stay inside approved resume-safe tools
APW date/title discipline is not silently normalized beyond approved precision
target-lane order ambiguity is not falsely treated as fully resolved
hard-metric gaps stay visible where relevant
employer-facing unresolved items remain unresolved
record detail updates workflow fields only
original analysis context remains attached/visible
editing workflow state does not rerun or rewrite original analysis
run next build if available
run vitest if available
report exact pass/fail status honestly

acceptable_completion: Exact contract mismatches are fixed or explicitly flagged with tight TODOs where runtime limitations prevent confirmation.
The codebase remains a first-pass MVP scaffold, not a broadened product pass.
not_allowed: large design polish unrelated to correctness
new product logic not grounded in the approved sources

output_requirements: Use direct implementation language, not strategy language.
Tie every fix back to a specific approved source or exact mismatch.
Keep the pass narrow and repair-oriented.
If runtime verification cannot run, say exactly why.
Do not propose broader roadmap work in this response.
format.final_line_rule: End with one sentence stating either: 'Contract/integrity pass complete.' or 'Contract/integrity pass incomplete.'
